description:
You are the system administrator of several application servers.  
Audit scripts generate daily textual logs and place yesterday’s files in:

/home/user/server_logs/

Three log files are already present in that directory when the session starts, each one using exactly this timestamped line format:

YYYY-MM-DD␣HH:MM:SS␣LEVEL␣Message...

LEVEL is always upper–case (e.g. INFO, WARN, ERROR).

Your objective is to transform those raw logs into three structured artefacts under a new folder named /home/user/output/ (create the folder if it does not yet exist).

1. Summarised CSV  
   • Path: /home/user/output/error_summary.csv  
   • Charset: UTF-8, Unix line endings (\n) only.  
   • Comma-separated, no surrounding spaces.  
   • First row is the exact literal header:  
     filename,error_count,first_error_time,last_error_time  
   • One subsequent row per *.log file, ordered **alphabetically by file name**.  
   • Column definitions:  
     - filename          : just the base file name (e.g. serverA.log).  
     - error_count       : integer number of lines containing the string “ERROR”.  
     - first_error_time  : timestamp of the first ERROR line in that file as it appears in the log; if there are no ERROR lines leave the field completely empty.  
     - last_error_time   : timestamp of the last ERROR line; leave empty if no ERROR lines.  
   • Example of a valid row (spacing must match exactly; no quotes):  
     serverX.log,4,2024-06-08 03:14:26,2024-06-08 06:51:09

2. Detailed JSON  
   • Path: /home/user/output/error_details.json  
   • UTF-8, pretty-printed with an indentation of exactly two spaces.  
   • The file must contain a single top-level JSON object whose keys are the individual log file names (alphabetical order).  
   • The value for each key is an array that holds every ERROR line **verbatim** (no leading/trailing whitespace removed, exactly as it appears in the source file, minus the final newline).  
   • Example structural template:  
     {  
       "serverA.log": [  
         "2024-01-02 10:00:00 ERROR Something broke"  
       ],  
       "serverB.log": []  
     }

3. Transformation log  
   • Path: /home/user/output/transformation.log  
   • Plain-text, UTF-8, Unix line endings.  
   • Exactly one line per processed log file, still in alphabetical order, following this rigid pattern (pay attention to spaces and the final “s”):  
     Processed <filename> : <N> errors found  
     Example: `Processed serverA.log : 2 errors found`

Acceptance tests will confirm:

• The three files exist at the exact paths above.  
• error_summary.csv contains the correct rows in the correct order and the fields follow the specification.  
• error_details.json respects the prescribed JSON structure and holds the precise ERROR lines.  
• transformation.log exists and its lines match the required wording and order.  

Hints (not commands): you will probably iterate over “*.log” files, grep for “ERROR”, count matches, extract timestamps with cut/awk/sed, and assemble the outputs with printf or a small script. Make sure your CSV has **no trailing spaces** and an ending newline is acceptable but not required.
truth:
Pre-existing log directory and exact file contents (each line terminated with \n):

/home/user/server_logs/serverA.log
2024-07-20 08:11:54 INFO Boot sequence initiated
2024-07-20 08:12:13 ERROR Disk sda IO failure
2024-07-20 08:12:15 ERROR Disk sda IO failure resolved
2024-07-20 08:13:00 INFO Service started

/home/user/server_logs/serverB.log
2024-07-20 09:00:00 INFO CRON daily job started
2024-07-20 09:00:02 WARN Low entropy
2024-07-20 09:00:24 ERROR Backup failed: remote host unreachable
2024-07-20 09:00:30 INFO CRON daily job finished

/home/user/server_logs/serverC.log
2024-07-20 10:42:11 INFO Healthcheck OK
2024-07-20 10:45:32 INFO Healthcheck OK

Expected artefacts after successful task execution:

/home/user/output/error_summary.csv
filename,error_count,first_error_time,last_error_time
serverA.log,2,2024-07-20 08:12:13,2024-07-20 08:12:15
serverB.log,1,2024-07-20 09:00:24,2024-07-20 09:00:24
serverC.log,0,,

/home/user/output/error_details.json
{
  "serverA.log": [
    "2024-07-20 08:12:13 ERROR Disk sda IO failure",
    "2024-07-20 08:12:15 ERROR Disk sda IO failure resolved"
  ],
  "serverB.log": [
    "2024-07-20 09:00:24 ERROR Backup failed: remote host unreachable"
  ],
  "serverC.log": []
}

/home/user/output/transformation.log
Processed serverA.log : 2 errors found
Processed serverB.log : 1 errors found
Processed serverC.log : 0 errors found
name: task_000000_63154f0f
